title: Mellow Cozy Korean Frozen Mix Wrap
description: Funky, gooey, cozy Korean dish featuring frozen seafood mix and nuka-pickle, served in a ti-leaf wrap. Perfect for a weeknight dinner or a snack.
instructions: Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Spread the frozen seafood mix on the baking sheet in a single layer.
Bake for 10 minutes or until thawed and lightly browned.
In a small bowl, whisk together the nuka-pickle brine, gochujang, soy sauce, and sesame oil.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked seafood mix, nuka-pickled carrots, nuka-pickled daikon, toasted sesame seeds, and scallions.
Add the toasted garlic and sesame oil mixture to the bowl and toss to combine.
Warm the ti-leaf wraps by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20-30 seconds.
Assemble the wraps by spooning the seafood mixture onto the center of each wrap and serving immediately.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
